text: Bridge information‚Üíreal world: give wrapped agents authorized real-world action tools so goals can leave the codebase
description: Aimparency's core promise is helping anyone reach high-level real-world goals ‚Äî e.g. build a house: contact contractors, compare quotes, schedule ‚Äî not only self-development. Today the wrapped agents act only in the information/code world; the system is entirely dogfooded on itself. The frontier capability is real-world action behind human-authorized, auditable tools: email/messaging, web forms, calendar, payments. Driving ONE non-software goal end-to-end through the graph + agents would validate the general hypothesis beyond dogfooding. Credentials/authorization make parts of this human-dependent ‚Äî pair tool integrations with clear human-setup aims.

status.comment: Direction-setting per Felix: the product's main idea is reaching real-world goals, not just self-dev. Needs human decision on which action surface (email/web/calendar) to start with + credential setup.
